content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660        322 5901         322 6441        322 6444

January 13, 1978


Marilyn Sue Barton
Barton and Young
16418 Clark Avenue
P.O. Box 1116
Bellflower, CA 90706

Dear Ms. Barton:

The Fair Political Practices Commission has received
your letter dated December 19, 1977, requesting written
advice pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329. I hope
the following advice will be helpful. However, since the
events in question occurred in 1976, the following advice
cannot constitute a complete defense in any enforcement
proceeding initiated by the Commission nor can it be
evidence of good faith conduct in any civil or criminal
proceeding. The Commission's advice can only constitute
such a defense or evidence good faith conduct if the advice
is sought prior to the alleged violation. See 2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 18329(e).

As I understand the facts, you were a candidate in 1976
for the office of Office No. 1 of the Municipal Court Judge,
Los Cerritos Judicial District. The incumbent judge had
retired effective March 1, 1976. A committee to support your
candidacy, Committee to Elect Barton ("the Committee"), was
formed on April 27, 1976. On May 5, 1976, the Governor
appointed J. Kimball Walker to fill the vacancy created by
the incumbent judge's resignation.

At the June 8, 1976, election, you received 23,643 votes.
In connection with the June 8, 1976, election, the Committee
filed its two preelection and one post-election campaign
statements on April 27, June 1 and August 10, 1976, respectively.

The Los Angeles County Registrar, the Los Angeles County
Board of Supervisors and the California Secretary of State
refused to certify the June 8, 1976, election. You filed a
petition directly with the California Supreme Court to
determine the validity of the election. You incurred and paid

Marilyn Sue Barton                          January 13, 1978
Page Two


all litigation expenses personally. On December 20, 1976,
the California Supreme Court decided the election was invalid.

Between September and December of 1976, the Committee
received contributions to defray the costs of this litigation.
Those funds have remained in the Committee's checking account.
The funds received after the post-election campaign statement
was filed have never been reported.

You now intend to be a candidate for a Municipal Court
judicial position in 1978. You asked (1) whether the 1976
funds may be used in the 1978 campaign; (2) whether the
Committee is required to file any campaign statements other
than in connection with the 1978 election and (3) if other
campaign statements are required, what they are.

There is nothing in the Political Reform Act which
restricts or prevents the use of the 1976 funds in your
1978 campaign.

Since the Committee was your controlled committee, all
of the contributions received by it between September and
December of 1976 were reportable as contributions. 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18215(b)(2). Accordingly the Committee
should have filed a semiannual campaign statement on Form
420 no later than January 31, 1977.

That semiannual campaign statement should have reported
all contributions received and expenditures made during the
period beginning with the day after the closing date of your
post-election campaign statement through December 31, 1976.
Since no campaign statements were filed in 1976 after the
August 10, 1976, post-election campaign statement, the
Committee will have to file that semiannual campaign state-
ment disclosing the unreported 1976 activity, in addition
to the campaign statements filed in connection with your
1978 candidacy.1/

The semiannual campaign statement should be filed with
the same filing officer with whom the Committee filed its
campaign statements in connection with the 1976 election.
The Committee's obligation to cumulate contributions and
expenditures over more than one reporting period terminates
upon the filing of a post-election campaign statement. See
Government Code Sections 82018 and 84210. However, the
semiannual campaign statement is not a post-election statement

1/ Based on the information you provided, it does not appear
that the Committee had any activity in 1977. Therefore the
Committee would not have an obligation to file any semiannual
campaign statements for 1977.

Marilyn Sue Barton                                January 13, 1978
Page Three


     for purposes of cumulation. The cumulative total amount
of contributions and expenditures reported on the semi-
annual statement will have to be carried over onto Column
A of the Summary Page of the first preelection campaign
statement the Committee files in 1978. Likewise the
Committee will have to cumulate the contributions received
in the last half of 1976 with any contributions received in
1978 from the same donor to determine whether the contri-
bution has to be itemized on the Committee's 1978 campaign
statements.

     I am enclosing several copies of Form 420. I am
also enclosing the 1978 Information Manual on Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act. Since
there have been no changes in the reporting requirements
for semiannual campaign statements, I am enclosing the most
recent Manual.

     If you have any questions concerning this advice, please
contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara Campbell
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division


Enclosures

LAW OFFICES

BARTON AND YOUNG

MARILYN SUE BARTON
LAWRENCE R. YOUNG

1642 CLARK AVENUE
POST OFFICE BOX 1116

BELLFLOWER, CALIFORNIA 90706
TELEPHONE (213) 925-6584 - 867-7238
[DAY OR NIGHT]

December 19, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Department
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Committee To Elect Barton
#760611

Gentlemen:

During February, 1976, Judge Roberta Butzbach was the incumbent
judge of Office No. 1 of the Municipal Court, LosCerritos Judicial
District. Her term was to expire in 1975 and she filed a declaration
of intent to stand for re-election. However, she failed to
perfect her Candidacy and on February 25, 1976, announced her
retirement effective March 1, 1976.

As a result of her retirement, I filed appropriate nominating
papers for the term to commence January 3, 1977 and was certified
as a candidate for the position. The Committee To Elect Barton
was formed April 27 and issued the above referenced Committee
Identification number by the Secretary of State.

On May 5 the Governor appointed J. Kimball Walker to fill the
interim vacancy created by Judge Butzbach's retirement. On
June 8, 1976, I prevailed at the election by a vote of 23,643
to 17,574. The Los Angeles County Registrar, Board of Supervisors
and the California Secretary of State refused to certify the
election.

The Committee To Elect Barton filed all campaign statements as
instructed by the County Registrar's office and required by law;
i.e., on April 27, 1976, on June 1, 1976 and on August 10, 1976.

In the meantime, I filed a petition for writ of mandate directly
with the California Supreme Court and incurred substantial liability
for costs of litigation. Between September, 1976 and the date
for filing the final campaign report, and December, 1976, additional
contributions were received by the Committee to defray those costs.
The receipts were deposited in the Committee checking account and
still remain there in tact. I paid all litigation expenses
personally.

The Supreme Court determined that the election was invalid (Barton
v. Panish, 135 Cal. Rptr 65) on December 20, 1976.

continued . . .

Fair Political Practices Commission
December 19, 1977
Page 2

All activity of the Committee occurred during 1976.

All funds received or spent by the Committee prior to the due
date of the post election campaign statement were reported.

All funds received after the final report was filed (August 10,
1976) were deposited and remain in the Committee account.

I intend to be a candidate for a Municipal Court judicial position
in 1978. May I please have your written advice pursuant to
Administrative Code Section 18329 as to the following:

     1. May the funds in question be used in the 1978
        campaign?

     2. Is a Committee Campaign report required prior
        to the first filing period of the 1978 Campaign?

     3. If the answer to 2 above is "yes", please advise
        of the nature of such report and provide necessary
        forms and instructions.

Your kind assistance in this matter will be greatly appreciated
at your earliest opportunity.

                                        Yours very truly,

                                        [signature]

                                        MARILYN SUE BARTON

MSB/sd

sections.question: You asked whether the 1975 funds may be used in the 1978 campaign; (2) whether the Committee is required to file any campaign statements other than in connection with the 1978 election and if it other campaign statements are required, what may apply. May the funds in question be used in the 1978 campaign? Is a committee Campaign report required prior to the first filing period of the 1978 Campaign? If the answer to 2 above is "yes", please advise of the nature of such report and provide necessary forms and instructions.
sections.conclusion: There is nothing in the Political Reform Act which restricts or prevents the use of the 1976 funds in your 1978 campaign. Since the committee was your controlled committee, all or the contributions received by it between September and December of 1976 were reportable as contributions. According to the commission the committee should have filed a semiannual campaign statement on Form 420 no later than January 31, 1977. The committee will have to file that semiannual campaign statement disclosing the unreported 1976 activity, in addition to the campaign statements filed in connection with your 1978 candidacy.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether funds received by the Committee to Elect Barton between September and December 1976 may be used in the candidate's 1978 campaign, and whether the committee must file additional campaign statements beyond those required for the 1978 election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the 1976 funds may be used in the 1978 campaign without restriction. However, the committee must file a semiannual campaign statement to report the previously unreported contributions received between September and December 1976, in addition to the statements required for the 1978 election.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated December 19, 1977) and the FPPC response letter (dated January 13, 1978). OCR quality issues present with some date formatting errors (e.g., '1375' for '1978' appears in document). The letter discusses timing restrictions on FPPC advice retroactively applied to 1976 events. Key dates appear inconsistent in document (some references to 1975 vs 1976) but core legal analysis is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked whether the 1975 funds may be used in the 1978 campaign; (2) whether the Committee is required to file any campaign statements other than in connection with the 1978 election and if it other campaign statements are required, what may apply. May the funds in question be used in the 1978 campaign? Is a committee Campaign report required prior to the first filing period of the 1978 Campaign? If the answer to 2 above is "yes", please advise of the nature of such report and provide necessary forms and instructions.

CONCLUSION: There is nothing in the Political Reform Act which restricts or prevents the use of the 1976 funds in your 1978 campaign. Since the committee was your controlled committee, all or the contributions received by it between September and December of 1976 were reportable as contributions. According to the commission the committee should have filed a semiannual campaign statement on Form 420 no later than January 31, 1977. The committee will have to file that semiannual campaign statement disclosing the unreported 1976 activity, in addition to the campaign statements filed in connection with your 1978 candidacy.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises Marilyn Sue Barton regarding the use of funds received by her campaign committee after the 1976 election and campaign reporting requirements for her 1978 judicial candidacy. The commission concludes that the funds may be used in 1978 but requires filing a semiannual campaign statement to properly report the previously unreported 1976 contributions.
